0: Openrouter offers two distinct methodologies for routing and determining the
1: computational model used to process a given request.
3: Automated Model Selection: This function routes a request to an optimized
4: model chosen from a curated set of available models. The selection is
5: predicated on the specific content of the user's prompt. The identifier of the
6: model that ultimately processes the request is returned in the response's
13: Sequential Model Fallback: This mechanism provides operational redundancy
14: by allowing users to specify a hierarchical list of models. The system will first
15: attempt to process the request with the primary model designated in the
16: sequence. Should this primary model fail to respond due to any number of error
17: conditions--such as service unavailability, rate-limiting, or content filtering--the
18: system will automatically re-route the request to the next specified model in
19: the sequence. This process continues until a model in the list successfully
20: executes the request or the list is exhausted. The final cost of the operation
21: and the model identifier returned in the response will correspond to the model
27: OpenRouter offers a detailed leaderboard ( https://openrouter.ai/rankings) which ranks
